title: iris pallida var. cucullata
intro: Iris pallida var. cucullata, also known as the Dalmatian iris or the Cretan iris, is a rare and endangered variety of the iris plant, characterized by its distinctive, heart-shaped flowers in shades of blue to purple. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, due to its high content of iridin, a compound with sedative and vasodilatory effects. The health benefits of Iris pallida var. cucullata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in dried form as a calming and relaxing addition to sleep-promoting blends.
